title: viola rupestris subsp. orioli-bolosii
intro: Viola rupestris subsp. orioli-bolosii, also known as the alpine pansy, is a subspecies of Viola rupestris, a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic content. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Viola rupestris subsp. orioli-bolosii are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
